author: śrīla gaura⋅kiśoradāsa bābājī mahārāja
verses:
kothāya go premamayi rādhe rādhe
rādhe rādhe go, jaya rādhe rādhe

dekhā diye prāṇa rākha rādhe rādhe
tomāra kāṅgāla tomāya ḍāke rādhe rādhe

rādhe vṛndāvana⋅vilāsinī rādhe rādhe
rādhe kānumanomohinī rādhe rādhe

rādhe aṣṭasakhīra śiromaṇi rādhe rādhe
rādhe vṛṣabhānu⋅nandini rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) niyama ka’re sadāi ḍāke, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke keśī-ghāṭe,
ābāra ḍāke vaṁśī-vaṭe, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke nidhu-vane,
ābāra ḍāke kuñjavane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke rādhā-kuṇḍe,
ābāra ḍāke śyāma-kuṇḍe, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke kusuma-vane,
ābāra ḍāke govardhane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) ekabāra ḍāke tāla-vane,
ābāra ḍāke tamāla-vane, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) malina vasana diye gāya,
vrajera dhūlāya gaḍāgaḍi jāya, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) mukhe rādhā rādhā bale,
bhāse nayanera jale, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) vṛndāvane kuli kuli,
ke̐de beḍāya rādhā bali’, rādhe rādhe

(gosāñī) chāppānna daṇḍa rātri dine
jāne nā rādhā⋅govinda vine, rādhe rādhe

tārapara cāri-daṇḍa śuti’ thāke
svapane rādhā⋅govinda dekhe, rādhe rādhe
en_translation:
Where are You, O Rādhā, the embodiment of love? All glories to You!

By appearing before me, please save my life. Your destitute servant calls You.

Rādhā, who enjoys pastimes in Vṛndāvana! Rādhā, who enchants Kṛṣṇa's mind!

Rādhā, the crest-jewel of the eight principal companions! Rādhā, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu!

A great soul regularly and always calls out: "Rādhe! Rādhe!"

Sometimes he calls at Keśī-ghāṭa, and again he calls at Vaṁśīvaṭa.

Sometimes he calls in Nidhuvana, and again he calls in the forest groves.

Sometimes he calls at Rādhā-kuṇḍa, and again he calls at Śyāma-kuṇḍa.

Sometimes he calls in Kusumavana, and again he calls at Govardhana.

Sometimes he calls in Tālavana, and again he calls in Tamālavana.

With dirty clothes on his body, he rolls in the dust of Vraja.

With "Rādhā! Rādhā!" on his lips, he floats in the tears from his eyes.

From lane to lane in Vṛndāvana, he cries and wanders, calling Your name.

For fifty-six units of time, day and night, he knows nothing except Rādhā and Govinda.

After that, he sleeps for four units of time, and in his dreams he sees Rādhā and Govinda.
